title: Remove or hide glossary page
description: glossary.html is a placeholder. Remove it from the nav and either delete the file or hide it until there is real content.
close_reason: Removed glossary.html and glossary nav link from all pages.

title: Add table of contents to essay pages
description: Add a top-of-page table of contents to essay pages, inferred from H2 headings via JavaScript. Should be collapsible/minimal, matching the site's monospace aesthetic. Positioned below the essay header, above the essay body. Style with IBM Plex Mono to match nav elements.

title: Multi-version essay sync: compile all snapshots in a lineage
description:
Rework the publish pipeline to sync an entire knots lineage rather than a single snapshot.

Current state: codex-ykf added a single-shot workflow_dispatch that fetches one publish ref and compiles it.

New behavior:
- git ls-remote to list all refs/knots/publish/{lineage}/* snapshots
- Skip snapshots already compiled (immutable — compile once)
- Compile each new snapshot to /essays/{id}/{version}/
- Latest (by manifest published_at) also served at /essays/{id}/
- Registry (_data/essays.json) tracks all versions per essay, not just current
- Essay template gets version nav (link to other versions)

Knots model (from knots agent):
- Snapshot name = version identifier (e.g. v1.0)
- manifest.json has published_at timestamp for ordering
- manifest.json has parent field linking to previous snapshot
- Snapshots are immutable once published
- No 'latest' pointer — use published_at to determine latest

Touches: workflow, compile script, update_registry, registry schema, essay template.

title: Build essay preprocessor
description: Create build/preprocess.py (stdlib only). Transforms paper_v2.md into Pandoc-ready markdown: sentence IDs ([f8fb] -> hidden span anchors), section markers (`[pa]` -> section-break divs), YAML frontmatter merging from paper.yaml. Must handle all 132 sentence IDs and 7 section markers in the source essay.
close_reason: Not needed — source repo renderer handles sentence IDs and section markers

title: Create archive page for historical essays
description: Create an archive page listing prefatory/historical works from the site's early days: 'decadence, extinction, whites: allegory' (Google Drive PDF), 'Autologist Fragments' (Google Doc), and the embedded essay (Google Doc iframe). Include cypherdude.jpeg as bottom art (resources/cypherdude.jpeg, CSS already in site.css as .bottom-art-img). Link from nav. These are external links, not knots-managed publications.
close_reason: Archive page created with two historical essay links and cypherdude bottom art. Nav links added to all pages.

title: Configure deploy key for source_engineering repo
description: Generate ed25519 deploy key. Add public key as read-only deploy key on codex-journal/source_engineering. Add private key as SOURCE_ENGINEERING_DEPLOY_KEY secret on codex-journal/codex-journal.github.io. The Action fetches refs/knots/publish/ from source_engineering — read-only access to publish refs only, no access to private source branches needed. Manual setup in GitHub UI.
close_reason: Deploy key configured manually in GitHub UI

title: Add nav links to essay pages
description: Essay pages (essays/*/index.html) only have Glossary in nav. Need to add Feeds and Archive links to match the rest of the site. Currently generated by Pandoc template — need to update the template or post-process.
close_reason: Created essays.html page with template + build integration. Updated nav site-wide (Essays, Glossary, Feeds, Archive) on all pages: index-template, feeds-template, glossary, archive, and essay pages.

title: Add GitHub activity feed to index page
description: Display recent GitHub activity on the index page. Fetch via RSS/Atom feed at build time (not client-side JS) and bake into static HTML. build_index.py pulls the feed, renders a recent activity section below the essay listing. Rebuilt on cron schedule (daily) or on push via GitHub Actions. Same build step that regenerates the index from the essay registry.
close_reason: Implemented multi-feed system: _data/feeds.json registry, build_index.py fetches Atom+RSS, index has collapsible 5-item preview, dedicated /feeds.html with per-group sections (Codex Journal, Source Engineering). Dynamic feed titles from XML.

title: Clean up index page placeholder
description: The live index.html shows [Title] and [Author] placeholders in the essay listing. Either generate the listing from _data/essays.json via build_index.py, or hide/remove the placeholder entry until the first essay is actually published.

title: Build essay registry and index generator
description: Create _data/essays.json registry format, build/update_registry.py (stamps version/date/ref into registry), and build/build_index.py (generates index.html landing page from registry listing published essays with title, version, date, link).

title: Create Pandoc template and build config
description: Create build/essay-template.html (Pandoc HTML template wrapping essay in site nav, header, body, references, footer) and build/essay-defaults.yaml (CSL style, link-citations, reference-section-title). Download and commit chicago-author-date.csl. Input is rendered .md from knots publish ref (refs/knots/publish/{lineage}/{snapshot_name}/), not raw source. Test full local Pandoc pipeline with a publish artifact.

title: Set up GitHub Action for essay publishing
description: Create .github/workflows/publish-essay.yml with workflow_dispatch trigger. Inputs: essay_id, lineage, snapshot_name. Steps: fetch refs/knots/publish/{lineage}/{snapshot_name}/ from source_engineering repo via deploy key, read manifest.json for metadata (state_root, parent, artifact hashes), run Pandoc with citeproc on the .md artifact, update _data/essays.json registry, regenerate index, commit compiled output. No private source checkout needed — only fetches immutable publish artifacts.
